name: Puente de las Tres Provincias — circular gravel from the Nacimiento del Río Tajo
name_original: Puente de las Tres Provincias — circular de gravel desde el Nacimiento del Río Tajo
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Albarracín traverse a region characterized by the Sierra de Albarracín mountain range, featuring diverse landscapes from rugged peaks to serene valleys. The area includes the Protected Landscape of Los Pinares del Rodeno, known for its red sandstone formations and pine forests. The Guadalaviar River meanders through the terrain, offering scenic paths along its banks. This varied topography provides a range of cycling experiences, from paved roads to unpaved segments with significant elevation changes. Best touring cycling routes around Albarracín The most popular touring cycling route is The Three Taifas Trail, a 30.1 miles (48.5 km) trail that takes 4 hours 1 minute to complete. This difficult route offers views of the Sierra de Albarracín and its medieval villages. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Puente de las Tres Provincias — circular gravel from the Nacimiento del Río Tajo, a moderate 19.1 miles (30.7 km) path. This route is a circular gravel ride starting from the source of the Tagus river. Local touring cyclists also love the Albarracín – Carretera del Canto loop from Albarracín, a 22.5 miles (36.2 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 24 minutes. Touring cycling around Albarracín is defined by mountain ranges, river valleys, and red sandstone formations. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to more demanding routes. The routes in Albarracín are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 390 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Albarracín's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Albarracín?
A: There are over 25 touring cycling routes around Albarracín documented on komoot. These routes cater to various skill levels, ranging from easy to very difficult, allowing cyclists to explore the diverse landscapes of the Sierra de Albarracín.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Albarracín?
A: Yes, there are routes suitable for those seeking an easier ride. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31844257" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bike loop from Albarracín&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 11.3 km route with a manageable elevation gain of around 240 meters, making it a good option for less experienced cyclists or families. The region's varied terrain ensures there are options for different ability levels.

Q: Can I find circular touring cycling routes in Albarracín?
A: Absolutely. Many touring cycling routes in Albarracín are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1094016335/puente-de-las-tres-provincias-circular-gravel-from-the-nacimiento-del-rio-tajo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de las Tres Provincias — circular gravel from the Nacimiento del Río Tajo&lt;/a&gt;, which is a 30.7 km gravel ride, and the more challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31511598" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albarracín – Carretera del Canto loop from Albarracín&lt;/a&gt;, covering 36.2 km.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around Albarracín?
A: The terrain around Albarracín is quite varied. You'll find routes with mostly paved surfaces, such as parts of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6929816" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portal de Molina, Albarracín – Main Square loop from Albarracín&lt;/a&gt;. However, many routes also feature unpaved segments, including gravel paths, especially those traversing the natural landscapes like the Protected Landscape of Los Pinares del Rodeno. The region's geological diversity includes red sandstone, limestone, and calcareous rock formations.

Q: When is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Albarracín?
A: The months of May and June are particularly popular for cycling in the Albarracín area. During this period, the weather is generally favorable, offering pleasant temperatures for exploring the region's diverse landscapes, from rugged peaks to serene valleys.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while cycling around Albarracín?
A: Albarracín's touring cycling routes offer stunning natural and historical sights. You can cycle through the unique red sandstone formations and pine forests of the Protected Landscape of Los Pinares del Rodeno. The town itself is situated in a meander of the Guadalaviar River, with picturesque views. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e956609247/the-three-taifas-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Three Taifas Trail&lt;/a&gt; offer views of the Sierra de Albarracín and its medieval villages. You can also explore the historic center of Albarracín with its distinctive reddish buildings and ancient defensive walls.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by waterfalls or lakes?
A: While specific cycling routes may not directly pass every water feature, the Sierra de Albarracín region is rich in water sources. You can find impressive waterfalls like El Salto de San Pedro in El Vallecillo and Cascada de Calomarde, as well as lagoons such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/756481" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de Bezas&lt;/a&gt; and Laguna de Noguera. These natural attractions are often accessible via nearby trails, which can be combined with cycling excursions.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Albarracín?
A: The touring cycling routes in Albarracín are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 30 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the stunning views of the Sierra de Albarracín, and the blend of natural beauty with historical charm found in the medieval villages.

Q: Is it possible to combine cycling with exploring historical sites in Albarracín?
A: Yes, Albarracín is an ideal location to combine cycling with cultural exploration. The town itself is a National Monument, featuring a historic center with unique architecture, the Albarracín Cathedral, and the Plaza Mayor. Many cycling routes offer views of medieval villages, and you can easily detour to explore the town's ancient defensive walls and the Torre del Andador for panoramic views after your ride.

Q: Are there options for more challenging touring cycling routes in Albarracín?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, Albarracín offers several difficult routes with significant elevation changes. For example, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e956609247/the-three-taifas-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Three Taifas Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a 48.5 km route with nearly 900 meters of elevation gain. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6929816" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portal de Molina, Albarracín – Main Square loop from Albarracín&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 58.9 km and features over 950 meters of ascent.

Q: What are some unique geological features to look out for while cycling?
A: The region is renowned for its unique geological formations, particularly within the Protected Landscape of Los Pinares del Rodeno. Here, you'll encounter enormous red sandstone rocks with capricious shapes, which contrast beautifully with the green pine forests. These formations are a result of the area's diverse geology, including red sandstone, sandstone, limestone, and calcareous rock.

Q: Is there parking available near the starting points of touring cycling routes?
A: Albarracín is a popular destination, and while specific parking details for every route start point are not always listed, the town and its surrounding areas generally offer parking options for visitors. It's advisable to check local signage or municipal information for the most convenient parking areas, especially when starting a route directly from Albarracín's historic center.
tips:
The official source of water is located at Fuente de García, in the Montes Universales, within the municipality of Frías de Albarracín (Teruel). It is located at an altitude of approximately 1,590 meters, surrounded by pine forests and high mountain meadows.
Access is very easy and well signposted. Take the A-1704 highway, which connects Frías de Albarracín with Huélamo (Cuenca). From the highway, a paved detour of approximately 2 km takes you directly to the recreational area and the monument. There is free parking next to the monument.
It is a beautiful picnic area with wooden tables surrounded by trees next to the Tagus River and the border between the provinces of Cuenca and Teruel.

The Puerto de El Cubillo is a mountain pass located in the Montes Universales, in the region of Castilla-La Mancha. This port can be divided into two phases: a first one with a certain difficulty that goes from the beginning, at the intersection of the CM-2105 road, until kilometer 5.5, and then a practically flat phase that ends at the border of the provinces of Cuenca and Teruel.
It is an ideal place for lovers of cycling and hiking, offering spectacular views and an impressive natural environment.😎🚴🚵🚶‍♂️🌄
El Monolito forest shelter. Ideal place to spend the night indoors. There are no services other than a sleeping room and a common space to cook. Keep it clean and don't leave trash.

waypoints.name: Forest Shelter and Picnic Area by the Tagus River
